name: Flipkart
overview_text: Flipkart is an Indian e-commerce company that was founded in 2007 by Sachin Bansal, Binny Bansal, and others. It allows consumers to shop online and have products delivered at their doorstep.

core_value_proposition: To provide a seamless and secure shopping experience to customers

safety_policies: Company has a comprehensive safety policy for employees

description: Flipkart is an Indian e-commerce company that was founded in 2007 by Sachin Bansal, Binny Bansal, and others. It allows consumers to shop online and have products delivered at their doorstep.
vision_statement: To make e-commerce accessible to every consumer in India by leveraging technology.
mission_statement: To be the most customer-centric company offering a wide selection of products and services to our customers.

core_values.Customer Obsession: Delighting customers by offering a wide selection of products and services.
core_values.Ownership: Taking ownership of our work and being accountable for our actions.
core_values.Innovation: Innovating and experimenting to stay ahead of the competition.
core_values.Long-term thinking: Focusing on long-term growth and sustainability.
core_values.Trust: Building trust with our customers, employees, and partners.

sales_motion: Flipkart's sales motion involves a multi-channel approach, including online advertising, social media marketing, and partnerships with influencers and other businesses.
exit_strategy_history: Flipkart was acquired by Walmart in 2018 for $16 billion.
carbon_footprint: Flipkart aims to be carbon neutral by 2030 and has set a target to reduce its carbon footprint by 50% by 2025.
ethical_sourcing: Flipkart has a code of conduct that outlines its expectations for suppliers regarding labor, human rights, and environmental practices.

case_studies.title: Flipkart's Supply Chain Optimization
case_studies.description: Flipkart optimized its supply chain by implementing a more efficient logistics system, resulting in a 25% reduction in costs.

case_studies.title: Flipkart's Digital Payments Platform
case_studies.description: Flipkart launched a digital payments platform that allowed customers to make payments online, resulting in a 30% increase in sales.

sustainability_csr: Flipkart has a sustainability and CSR strategy that focuses on reducing its environmental impact and promoting social responsibility.
crisis_behavior: Flipkart has a crisis management plan in place that includes procedures for responding to customer complaints, product recalls, and other crises.
